rationale: The citing paper uses Hamilton and Srivastava (2008) for a specific prior result in partitioned pricing, namely that pricing the higher-consumption-value component higher can improve overall price evaluations. It then positions trade-ins as a related but distinct two-component pricing setting and builds its own theory around that distinction (one component is a gain, the other a loss). This is more than a passing mention because the cited study helps frame the conceptual setup and contribution of the trade-in model.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the partitioned-pricing line represented by Hamilton and Srivastava (2008) to trade-in transactions. It keeps the component-pricing perspective but adds a prospect-theory gain/loss structure and predicts a ratio-based reversal between preferences for overpayment and underpayment.
